subject: SCIENCE
title: Niko’s Plant Investigation
objective: Explain plant needs and use fair-test observations as evidence.
story: Niko compares a green pechay plant near a bright window with a pale, drooping plant in a dark corner and asks whether sunlight or water explains the difference.

steps.prompt: Niko notices two different-looking pechay plants.
steps.content: Niko compares a green pechay plant near a bright window with a pale, drooping plant in a dark corner and asks whether sunlight or water explains the difference.
steps.accessibilityNarration: Niko compares a green pechay plant near a bright window with a pale, drooping plant in a dark corner and asks whether sunlight or water explains the difference.

steps.prompt: Plants need suitable light and water. In a fair test, change one condition and keep the others the same.
steps.accessibilityNarration: A fair investigation changes one condition.

steps.prompt: Two seedlings get equal water. One gets daylight and one stays dark. Predict.
choices: The lighted plant will likely be greener
The dark plant will be greener
steps.accessibilityNarration: Two seedlings get equal water. One gets daylight and one stays dark. Predict.

steps.hint: Observations can be seen or measured.

steps.prompt: Water spills during a sunlight test. What should Niko do?
choices: Restore the planned water and record the spill
steps.accessibilityNarration: Water spills during a sunlight test. What should Niko do?

steps.prompt: Which condition changes when testing water?
steps.retryFeedback: Keep light, soil, and starting size the same.
steps.accessibilityNarration: Which condition changes when testing water?

steps.prompt: Complete: Plants need suitable ___ and ___. Evidence: ___.
steps.retryFeedback: Include sunlight, water, and an observation.
steps.accessibilityNarration: Complete: Plants need suitable ___ and ___. Evidence: ___.

assessment.prompt: Why do most green plants need sunlight?
choices: Leaves use light to help make food
assessment.retryFeedback: Light does not replace soil or water.
assessment.accessibilityNarration: Why do most green plants need sunlight?

assessment.prompt: Which may show a plant needs water?
assessment.retryFeedback: The cat and label are unrelated.
assessment.accessibilityNarration: Which may show a plant needs water?

assessment.prompt: Two similar seedlings get equal light; one gets no water. Predict.
choices: The watered seedling likely grows better
assessment.retryFeedback: Equal light does not remove the need for water.
assessment.accessibilityNarration: Two similar seedlings get equal light; one gets no water. Predict.

assessment.prompt: Explain why more water is not always better and describe a fair test.
correctAnswers: Too much can harm roots; vary only measured water
assessment.hint: Mention harm and one changed variable.
assessment.retryFeedback: Keep all conditions except water the same.
assessment.accessibilityNarration: Explain why more water is not always better and describe a fair test.
